text.title: LLM Cost-Quality-Latency Tradeoff Framework Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every LLM Cost-Quality-Latency Tradeoff Framework application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Build a 3D scatter (cost, latency, quality) for each candidate config: model, prompt, post-process. Pareto front is the only valid candidate set.
rule.rationale: Dominated configs (worse on every axis than another) should never ship; explicit Pareto culls them.

rule.statement: Escalate weak→strong model on low-confidence outputs (judge score, log-prob, retrieval miss). Do not escalate the entire workflow.
rule.rationale: Indiscriminate escalation wipes the cost benefit; confidence-gated escalation keeps savings.

rule.statement: Add second-pass (critic, judge, reranker) only when first-pass quality gap > acceptable threshold AND second-pass cost < (quality_gain × value_per_quality_point).
rule.rationale: Second passes feel valuable but often cost more than they earn; explicit criteria gate the add.

rule.statement: Add reranker when top-3 retrieval contains the answer but top-1 does not (retrieval mAP@3 - mAP@1 > 0.1). Otherwise reranking is overhead.
rule.rationale: Rerankers fix retrieval order; if retrieval is correct, rerank adds latency without quality gain.

rule.statement: Re-run the tradeoff analysis quarterly OR after every model major-version change. Pareto front shifts as new models ship.
rule.rationale: GPT-4 → GPT-5 / Claude 4 → 5 type updates can collapse the entire frontier; quarterly cadence captures.
